openmeter/billing/rating/service/mutator.purpose: Post- and pre-calculation mutation pipeline that transforms rating.DetailedLines after (or PricerCalculateInput before) a pricer runs. Each mutator applies one concern — min/max spend commitments, credits allocation, percentage discounts, usage-quantity discounts — keeping pricing logic composable and isolated.

patterns.name: Interface segregation by phase
patterns.description: Implement PostCalculationMutator (signature: Mutate(rate.PricerCalculateInput, rating.DetailedLines) (rating.DetailedLines, error)) for post-calculation changes, or PreCalculationMutator (Mutate(rate.PricerCalculateInput) (rate.PricerCalculateInput, error)) for pre-calculation input changes. Never conflate the two.

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion
patterns.description: Every mutator struct must have a blank-identifier assertion against its interface (var _ PostCalculationMutator = (*MyMutator)(nil)) immediately after the type declaration.

patterns.name: ChildUniqueReferenceID for deduplication
patterns.description: Every DetailedLine appended by a mutator must set ChildUniqueReferenceID using the constants in the rating package (rating.MinSpendChildUniqueReferenceID, rating.RateCardDiscountChildUniqueReferenceID, etc.) to allow idempotent re-calculation.

patterns.name: IsLastInPeriod / IsFirstInPeriod guards
patterns.description: Commitment and discount mutators that apply only once per billing period must guard on i.IsLastInPeriod() or i.IsFirstInPeriod() to avoid double-billing in progressive billing split-line scenarios.

patterns.name: CurrencyCalculator for rounding
patterns.description: All monetary arithmetic must use i.CurrencyCalculator.RoundToPrecision(...) rather than raw alpacadecimal operations to respect the invoice currency precision.

patterns.name: Error sentinel for over-allocated credits
patterns.description: If credits are not fully consumed after iterating all lines, return billing.ErrInvoiceLineCreditsNotConsumedFully — never silently discard the remaining credit value.

patterns.name: Usage discount idempotency via MergeDiscountsByChildUniqueReferenceID
patterns.description: DiscountUsage (PreCalculationMutator) rewrites the line's StandardLineDiscounts.Usage using MergeDiscountsByChildUniqueReferenceID so reruns stay idempotent; it always calls removeUsageDiscounts first to strip stale entries.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the two mutator interfaces (PostCalculationMutator, PreCalculationMutator). All other files implement one of these.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Do not add methods to either interface without updating every implementor.

key_file_guides.role: MinAmountCommitment and MaxAmountCommitment post-mutators. MinAmount appends a commitment DetailedLine; MaxAmount iterates lines and calls AddDiscountForOverage.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MinAmountCommitment reads i.GetPreviouslyBilledAmount() — the period coverage for the new line must span the full service period when progressively billed (i.FullProgressivelyBilledServicePeriod).

key_file_guides.role: Allocates pre-computed CreditsApplied across DetailedLines in order, appending CreditApplied entries per line.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Credits must be fully consumed; any positive remainder triggers ErrInvoiceLineCreditsNotConsumedFully.

key_file_guides.role: Applies a rate-card percentage discount to every line's TotalAmount as an AmountLineDiscountManaged entry. Requires a non-empty CorrelationID on the discount.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ChildUniqueReferenceID is built from rating.RateCardDiscountChildUniqueReferenceID format string — correlationID must be present or the function returns an error.

key_file_guides.role: Pre-mutator that reduces usage quantity based on rate-card usage discount. Not idempotent on the raw line — always strips old usage discounts before reapplying.
key_file_guides.watch_for: DiscountUsage manipulates PricerCalculateInput.Usage.Quantity directly; PreLinePeriodQuantity must be adjusted for already-consumed discount from earlier split lines.

anti_patterns: Adding state to mutator structs — all mutators are stateless value types; context must flow through PricerCalculateInput
Skipping ChildUniqueReferenceID on appended DetailedLines — breaks idempotent recalculation
Doing currency rounding with raw alpacadecimal arithmetic instead of CurrencyCalculator.RoundToPrecision
Applying min/max commitments outside of IsLastInPeriod guard — causes double-billing on progressive billing
Implementing both PreCalculationMutator and PostCalculationMutator in the same struct — keeps phase semantics clear

decisions.decision: Two separate interfaces (Pre vs Post) rather than a single pipeline step
decisions.rationale: Pre-mutators modify usage quantity before pricing (changing what gets priced), post-mutators modify resulting DetailedLines. Conflating them would force every mutator to understand both the pricer input and output shapes.

decisions.decision: Stateless value-type mutators with no constructor
decisions.rationale: All needed context is in PricerCalculateInput; stateless types allow safe reuse across concurrent calculations and require no DI wiring.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new PostCalculationMutator that caps line amounts

openmeter/billing/rating/service/rate.purpose: Concrete Pricer implementations — one per productcatalog price type (Flat, Unit, Dynamic, Package, Tiered/Graduated, Tiered/Volume) — plus shared billable-period resolution helpers. Each pricer converts a PricerCalculateInput into rating.DetailedLines and determines whether a line is billable at a given point in time.

patterns.name: Pricer interface implementation with compile-time assertion
patterns.description: Every pricer struct must satisfy the Pricer interface (GenerateDetailedLines + ResolveBillablePeriod) and declare var _ Pricer = (*MyPricer)(nil) after the type.

patterns.name: Embed base pricers for period resolution
patterns.description: Usage-based pricers that support progressive billing embed ProgressiveBillingMeteredPricer; pricers that never support split lines embed NonProgressiveBillingPricer. This provides the ResolveBillablePeriod method. Flat embeds neither and implements ResolveBillablePeriod directly.

patterns.name: ChildUniqueReferenceID constants from rating package
patterns.description: All DetailedLine entries must set ChildUniqueReferenceID using constants defined in the parent rating package: rating.UsageChildUniqueReferenceID, rating.FlatPriceChildUniqueReferenceID, rating.GraduatedTieredPriceUsageChildUniqueReferenceID, etc.

patterns.name: IsLastInPeriod / IsFirstInPeriod for payment term gating
patterns.description: InAdvance lines are emitted only when l.IsFirstInPeriod() is true; InArrears lines only when l.IsLastInPeriod() is true (or always for usage lines). This prevents duplicate billing across progressive split lines.

patterns.name: TieredPriceCalculatorInput for graduated tier math
patterns.description: Graduated tiered calculations use GraduatedTiered.TieredPriceCalculator with a TieredPriceCalculatorInput struct; the TierCallbackFn receives TierCallbackInput per tier boundary. Do not reimplement the range-splitting logic inline.
patterns.example: p.TieredPriceCalculator(TieredPriceCalculatorInput{TieredPrice: price, FromQty: usage.PreLinePeriodQuantity, ToQty: ..., TierCallbackFn: func(in TierCallbackInput) error {...}})

patterns.name: billing.ValidationError for invariant violations
patterns.description: Return billing.ValidationError{Err: ...} (not fmt.Errorf) when a line configuration violates an invariant (e.g. progressive billing on a flat-price line, unsupported payment term).

patterns.name: Tiered router delegates to volume/graduated sub-pricers
patterns.description: The Tiered struct is a router that inspects price.Mode and delegates to its volume or graduated sub-pricer. New tiered modes must be added to the switch in both GenerateDetailedLines and ResolveBillablePeriod.

key_file_guides.role: Defines Pricer interface and PricerCalculateInput (embeds rating.StandardLineAccessor + CurrencyCalculator + Usage + StandardLineDiscounts). IsLastInPeriod / IsFirstInPeriod helpers live here.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PricerCalculateInput.Usage is a pointer; call GetUsage() to get a safe copy — do not dereference directly.

key_file_guides.role: ProgressiveBillingMeteredPricer and NonProgressiveBillingPricer — shared ResolveBillablePeriod logic. Progressive truncates asOf to streaming.MinimumWindowSizeDuration.
key_file_guides.watch_for: NonProgressiveBillingPricer rejects lines with a SplitLineGroupID; embedding it in a new pricer type blocks progressive billing for that price type.

key_file_guides.role: GraduatedTiered and TieredPriceCalculator. splitTierRangeAtBoundary handles the edge case where FromQty/ToQty cut across tier boundaries for progressive billing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TieredPriceCalculatorInput.Validate() must pass before calling TieredPriceCalculator; both FromQty and ToQty must be non-negative and FromQty <= ToQty.

key_file_guides.role: Router that dispatches to volume/graduated based on price.Mode. Only entry point for external callers of tiered pricing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new tiered mode requires updates to both switch blocks.

key_file_guides.role: FlatPrice pricer with custom ResolveBillablePeriod that uses line.InvoiceAt instead of period end. Does not support progressive billing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Flat prices check invoiceAt vs asOf (both truncated); InAdvance emits only on IsFirstInPeriod, InArrears only on IsLastInPeriod.

anti_patterns: Reimplementing tier-boundary range splitting outside TieredPriceCalculator
Returning fmt.Errorf for billing invariant violations — use billing.ValidationError{Err: ...}
Setting ChildUniqueReferenceID as a raw string literal instead of using rating package constants
Adding progressive-billing support to a pricer that embeds NonProgressiveBillingPricer without switching the base
Calling PricerCalculateInput.Usage directly instead of GetUsage() — panics when Usage is nil

decisions.decision: Separate structs per price type rather than a single switch-heavy function
decisions.rationale: Each price type has distinct period-resolution semantics and line structure; separate structs allow independent testing and embedding of the correct base pricer.

decisions.decision: TieredPriceCalculator as a reusable callback-driven algorithm
decisions.rationale: Graduated tiered pricing requires range splitting across FromQty/ToQty boundaries; centralising the algorithm with TierCallbackFn avoids duplicating the range logic in both volume and graduated pricers.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new usage-based pricer for a hypothetical 'stepped' price type

openmeter/billing/rating/service/testuti.purpose: Test harness for pricer and mutator unit tests. Provides CalculationTestCase, line-mode factories (single-period, mid-period split, last-in-period), and RunCalculationTestCase which wires a real service.New() and compares JSON-serialised DetailedLines output.

patterns.name: RunCalculationTestCase as the single test entry point
patterns.description: All pricer/mutator tests call testutil.RunCalculationTestCase(t, CalculationTestCase{...}) — never instantiate service.New() directly in test files.
patterns.example: testutil.RunCalculationTestCase(t, testutil.CalculationTestCase{Price: ..., LineMode: testutil.SinglePerPeriodLineMode, Usage: ..., Expect: ...})

patterns.name: LineMode enum for split-line scenarios
patterns.description: Use the TestLineMode constants (SinglePerPeriodLineMode, MidPeriodSplitLineMode, LastInPeriodSplitLineMode) to control whether the test line has a SplitLineGroupID and how its period is positioned within TestFullPeriod.

patterns.name: JSON equality assertion
patterns.description: RunCalculationTestCase uses require.JSONEq on marshalled slices to avoid nil-vs-empty-slice false failures and to give readable diffs on mismatches.

patterns.name: PreviousBilledAmount for progressive billing tests
patterns.description: Set CalculationTestCase.PreviousBilledAmount to simulate already-billed amounts in previous split lines; the harness inserts a fake prior line with that Totals.Amount.

key_file_guides.role: Single file: defines CalculationTestCase, FeatureUsageResponse, TestLineMode, TestFullPeriod constant, and RunCalculationTestCase. Imports service.New() to run a real pipeline.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The harness always sets line name to 'feature'; expected DetailedLine.Name values in tests must match that prefix (e.g. 'feature: usage in period').

anti_patterns: Instantiating service.New() directly in *_test.go files — use RunCalculationTestCase instead
Constructing billing.StandardLine manually in tests outside this package — use the harness to avoid missing SplitLineHierarchy setup
Using require.Equal for DetailedLines slices — use RunCalculationTestCase which does JSON equality to handle nil/empty slice equivalence

decisions.decision: JSON equality rather than deep-equal for DetailedLines
decisions.rationale: DetailedLines contains alpacadecimal.Decimal fields whose zero values may differ between nil and empty; JSON normalises these and produces legible diffs in test output.
